shared_patterns.pattern: in a [descriptive phrase], [subject] [verb] [object]
the [noun] [verb] like [comparison], [adjective]-worthy
use of emotional language and imagery
repeated focus on character depth and emotional impact
use of complex sentence structures with multiple clauses
frequent use of commas to create lengthy, descriptive phrases
repetitive use of phrases that critique or analyze film elements
a tendency to include specific names or references to industry figures
a mix of formal and informal tones within the same sample
use of metaphorical language
emotional descriptors
humorous critiques
exaggerated expressions
conversational tone
use of vivid imagery
contrast between positive and negative sentiments
phrases that reflect on pacing or engagement
humorous or sarcastic commentary on performances
use of comparative phrases (e.g., 'very good (but not great)', 'formulaic and forgettable')
concise, formulaic structure
vague descriptors (e.g., 'substantial or fresh')
repeated use of adjectives to describe films
repeated use of short, punchy phrases
frequent use of commas in lists
similar sentence structures with a focus on emotional descriptors
use of contrasting ideas (e.g., funny in a sick, twisted sort of way)
tendency to use vague or abstract language
